name: Hebron Access (Steel Bridge)
facilities: Gravel parking lot, a few primitive campsites, gravel-bar launch; locally called 'Steel Bridge' (concrete slab bridge).
description: MDC access between Dora and Vanzant. MDC/float-guide directions: CR 279 north off Hwy 181. Reported thefts here per local news.

name: Highway 14 bridge (Twin Bridges) over North Fork
facilities: Public MoDOT highway bridge / roadside; used as put-in/take-out.
description: This is the PUBLIC Hwy 14 bridge coordinate. The private Twin Bridges Canoe & Campground resort sits at this crossing and is handled separately as a service. 'Twin Bridges' = the two adjacent Hwy 14 bridges (North Fork + Spring Creek).

name: North Fork Recreation Area (Hammond)
facilities: USFS campground (~19 sites), day-use/picnic area, non-motorized boat launch, vault toilets, drinking water. Also known as Hammond Camp.
description: Located at the Route CC crossing of the North Fork. Periodically closed for construction in recent years.

name: Blair Bridge Access
facilities: Concrete boat ramp, two campsites, campfire rings, picnic tables, privy. Concrete low-water bridge crossing.
description: Float guide lists mile 39.4. Access is on the right immediately after the low-water bridge on CR 354.

name: Patrick Bridge Access
facilities: Gravel canoe launch ramp, campsites, privies, Althea Spring Trail. Heavily used in summer.
description: Reported coordinate is the bridge crossing on-channel. Per floatmissouri the gravel ramp is on the NE side of the H bridge (~36.6436, -92.2222 per the OSM access node). Directions: Hwy 160 W from Caulfield ~3.5 mi, then Route H north 4 mi.

name: Highway PP bridge (North Fork high bridge)
facilities: Public MoDOT highway bridge. A low-water dam sits just downstream (a 'canoe-buster' — portage on the right).
description: Coordinate is the OSM bridge centerline (single coordinate source, but route-ref + waterway-name + float-guide together make the identification unambiguous). River meanders here: PP (mile 45.3) sits slightly south of the Tecumseh take-out because the river loops through Dawt (mile 47) before bending north into Norfork Lake.

name: Tecumseh / Norfork Lake Public Access
facilities: Public access near Hwy 160 at the upper end of Norfork Lake; final ~2 mi of the float is slackwater lake.
description: Pre-confirmed value VERIFIED via USGS. Ownership of the ramp itself unconfirmed (upper Norfork Lake is USACE-managed; may be MDC/USACE/county). Coordinate is the USGS gauge; verify the exact ramp if a distinct launch is needed.

name: Highway 76 bridge (upper put-in)
facilities: Public MoDOT highway bridge, used as the uppermost float put-in.
description: Bonus/upstream point. Coordinate from OSM only (single source) but unambiguous. This is the northernmost/top crossing near the Willow Springs headwaters.

name: Osborn Ford
facilities: Low-water bridge crossing used as informal float access.
description: NOTE: the task's '(Hwy CC)' label appears incorrect — the Route CC crossing is at the North Fork Recreation Area (mile 29.2), not at Osborn Ford (mile 12.2). Verified upper-river North Fork road crossings that are CANDIDATES for Osborn/Hale Ford but could not be name-matched: CR 281 at 36.9773, -92.1667 and CR 'E-276' at 36.9473, -92.2021 (both confirmed over the North Fork via Overpass). Left null to avoid guessing the name-to-coordinate mapping.

name: Hale Ford (Hale's Crossing)
facilities: Low-water bridge crossing used as informal float access.
description: Candidate verified North Fork crossings (unnamed match) same as Osborn Ford: CR 281 (36.9773, -92.1667) and CR E-276 (36.9473, -92.2021). Left null to avoid guessing.

name: Kelly Ford
facilities: Tiny, remote informal river access on private (Rainbow Springs) land.
description: Not on public land — tolerated informal use. Excluded from confident coordinates; likely should be treated cautiously (semi-private).
